description: Compile-only firmware validation targets (FW-COMPILE-MATRIX-001). Source-of-truth list of product YAMLs that CI may compile to validate YAML / config / codegen buildability before hardware is available. This file is a CI validation lane only: compile success is necessary but not sufficient for preview / stable readiness. It does not expose new WebFlash builds, create release artifacts, import firmware, promote any product, or add any product YAML. See docs/compile-only-firmware-validation.md.

targets.reason: Currently committed Release-One stable WebFlash build. Compile-only validation pins that the canonical Release-One YAML still composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version, independent of hardware availability.
targets.notes: Verified-for-release-one product (config/product-catalog.json status=production, webflash_build_matrix=true). This compile-only target does NOT add a new WebFlash exposure, flip webflash_build_matrix, or change the committed build entry; it only validates buildability.

targets.reason: Currently committed LED-bearing preview WebFlash build. Compile-only validation pins that the LED-bearing canonical YAML still composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version, independent of hardware availability. Compile success is not LED-stable proof; the LED stable gauntlet (S360-300-BENCH-001, WF-HW-TEST-001, WF-HW-TEST-003, RELEASE-007) remains open.
targets.notes: Preview-only product (config/product-catalog.json status=preview, channel=preview, webflash_build_matrix=true). This compile-only target does NOT promote the LED preview to stable, change the committed build entry, or claim hardware proof exists.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the minimum Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health package composition that the WebFlash-shipping Release-One build already proves compiles. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, not S360-410 schematic verification, not Release-One PoE caveat closure, and not product-catalog / stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate at tests/test_product_catalog.py::_top_level_product_yamls does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the Core ceiling + PoE PSU + RoomIQ (comfort_ceiling + presence_ceiling) package composition stripped of VentIQ. Same RoomIQ subset that the WebFlash-shipping Release-One build already exercises. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, and not product-catalog / stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json.

targets.reason: Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ TOP-LEVEL product-YAML compile-only validation target (V1-R4-CREATE-001). Registers the canonical RoomIQ-only PoE ceiling product YAML authored by V1-R4-CREATE-001 (products/sense360-ceiling-poe-roomiq.yaml; the S360-KIT-BEDROOM-P firmware deliverable, NOT the products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-roomiq.yaml skeleton) directly into the compile-only validation lane so the actual top-level product YAML carries its own compile-only coverage. This mirrors the FanDAC / FanPWM top-level precedent (ceiling-poe-fandac-product-compile-only / ceiling-poe-fanpwm-product-compile-only register their top-level product YAMLs alongside their skeletons). The product YAML composes the IDENTICAL Core ceiling + PoE PSU (S360-410) + base/health + RoomIQ (comfort_ceiling + presence_ceiling) package set as the already-registered skeleton target ceiling-poe-roomiq-compile-only; the same RoomIQ subset is exercised by the WebFlash-shipping Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ build. compile_validation_status is validated-full-compile: the hosted Preview Compile Dry-Run (.github/workflows/preview-compile-dryrun.yml; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; run 26821900127; 2026-06-02; ESPHome 2026.4.5) ran a real esphome compile dry-run against this product YAML and it passed (job 'Compile Dry-Run: Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ', conclusion success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; no compile is fabricated. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a PRODUCT-POE-410-001 / WEBFLASH-POE-410-001 / RELEASE-POE-410-001 unblock, NOT S360-410 schematic / BOM verification, NOT hardware proof, NOT compliance approval, and NOT stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Top-level Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ product-YAML compile-only target. product_yaml is products/sense360-ceiling-poe-roomiq.yaml (a top-level products/ product YAML with a config/product-catalog.json row from V1-R4-CREATE-001, status: blocked, blocker: PRODUCT-POE-410-001, webflash_build_matrix: false, no artifact_name, no webflash_wrapper). This is a SEPARATE registration from the ceiling-poe-roomiq-compile-only skeleton (products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-roomiq.yaml), which is preserved unchanged; both target the Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ config string but are distinct files with distinct ids. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records that the hosted Preview Compile Dry-Run (run 26821900127; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; 2026-06-02; ESPHome 2026.4.5) ran a real esphome compile dry-run against this product YAML and it passed (conclusion success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; no compile is fabricated. This is firmware-build proof only: NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT a PRODUCT-POE-410-001 unblock, NOT S360-410 schematic / BOM verification, NOT hardware proof, NOT compliance, and NOT stable / preview promotion. No WebFlash exposure, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no release artifact, no Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ entry in config/webflash-builds.json, not in release_one_required_configs. S360-410 schematic_status stays cataloged_unverified and the Release-One PoE caveat (PRODUCT-POE-410-001) remains open.

targets.reason: Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ TOP-LEVEL product-YAML compile-only validation target (RELEASE-PREVIEW-BUILD-FIXES-001). Registers the AirIQ + RoomIQ PoE ceiling product YAML authored by RELEASE-PREVIEW-BUILD-FIXES-001 (products/sense360-ceiling-poe-airiq-roomiq.yaml, a thin compat shim that !includes the bundle products/bundles/ceiling-poe-airiq-roomiq.yaml) into the compile-only validation lane so the metadata validator covers the concrete product YAML, not just the products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-airiq-roomiq.yaml skeleton. This mirrors the ceiling-poe-roomiq-product-compile-only / ceiling-usb-roomiq-product-compile-only precedent. The bundle composes Core ceiling (S360-100 board package) + PoE PSU (S360-410 board package) + AirIQ (S360-210 board package + airiq_basic_profile) + RoomIQ (S360-200 board package: comfort_ceiling + presence_ceiling + profiles) + base/health, with no VentIQ, no fan, and no LED; the identical composition is exercised by the skeleton target ceiling-poe-airiq-roomiq-compile-only. compile_validation_status is validated-full-compile: the hosted Preview Compile Dry-Run (.github/workflows/preview-compile-dryrun.yml; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; run 26821900127; 2026-06-02; ESPHome 2026.4.5) ran a real esphome compile dry-run against this product YAML and it passed (job 'Compile Dry-Run: Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ', conclusion success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; no compile is fabricated. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT a PRODUCT-POE-410-001 unblock, NOT S360-410 schematic / BOM verification, NOT AirIQ sensor-stack bench evidence, NOT hardware proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion.

This is firmware-build proof only: NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT a PRODUCT-POE-410-001 unblock, NOT S360-410 schematic / BOM verification, NOT hardware proof, NOT compliance, and NOT stable / preview promotion. No WebFlash exposure, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no release artifact, no Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ entry in config/webflash-builds.json, not in release_one_required_configs. S360-410 schematic_status stays cataloged_unverified and the Release-One PoE caveat (PRODUCT-POE-410-001) remains open.

targets.reason: Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED TOP-LEVEL product-YAML compile-only validation target (RELEASE-PREVIEW-BUILD-FIXES-001). Registers the RoomIQ + LED PoE ceiling product YAML authored by RELEASE-PREVIEW-BUILD-FIXES-001 (products/sense360-ceiling-poe-roomiq-led.yaml, a thin compat shim that !includes the bundle products/bundles/ceiling-poe-roomiq-led.yaml) into the compile-only validation lane so the metadata validator covers the concrete product YAML. Before this slice there was no dedicated YAML for the LED token — the preview manifest pointed at the products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-roomiq.yaml skeleton, which does not encode LED. The bundle composes Core ceiling (S360-100 board package) + PoE PSU (S360-410 board package) + RoomIQ (S360-200 board package: comfort_ceiling + presence_ceiling + profiles) + LED (S360-300 board package, WS2812B ceiling halo on GPIO38 per HW-010) + base/health, with no AirIQ, no VentIQ, and no fan. It is the LED-bearing sibling of the authored Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ build; the S360-300 LED board is the same ring the published Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ-LED preview ships. compile_validation_status is validated-full-compile: the hosted Preview Compile Dry-Run (.github/workflows/preview-compile-dryrun.yml; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; run 26821900127; 2026-06-02; ESPHome 2026.4.5) ran a real esphome compile dry-run against this product YAML and it passed (job 'Compile Dry-Run: Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED', conclusion success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; no compile is fabricated. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT a PRODUCT-POE-410-001 unblock, NOT S360-410 schematic / BOM verification, NOT an LED stable promotion (LED stays preview), NOT hardware proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion.

LED stays preview. No WebFlash exposure, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no release artifact, no Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED entry in config/webflash-builds.json, not in release_one_required_configs. targets.reason: FanRelay (S360-310) compile-only validation target (FW-COMPILE-RELAY-001). Validates that the PRODUCT-RELAY-001 / PR #564 product YAML (advanced / manual-warning-only sibling of Release-One; composes Core ceiling + PoE PSU + VentIQ + RoomIQ + FanRelay; inherits ${relay_pin} → GPIO3 from the parent Core abstract package post-CORE-ABSTRACT-BUS-001A) still composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a RELEASE-RELAY-001 unblock, NOT compliance approval, NOT hardware proof, NOT a competent-person sign-off, NOT a default kit / not recommended, and NOT stable / preview promotion. The advanced / manual-warning posture defined by PRODUCT-RELAY-001-READINESS-REFRESH / PR #563 and confirmed by WEBFLASH-RELAY-001-READINESS-REFRESH / PR #565 stays in force: WebFlash exposure remains blocked behind the seven remaining WebFlash gates owned by a separate WEBFLASH-RELAY-001 slice. This target sits in the compile-only validation lane only.
targets.notes: FanRelay product YAML lives at products/sense360-ceiling-poe-ventiq-fanrelay-roomiq.yaml (NOT under products/webflash/, NOT under products/compile-only/). The matching catalog row in config/product-catalog.json (config_string: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanRelay-RoomIQ, status: hardware-pending, webflash_build_matrix: false, no artifact_name, no webflash_wrapper) was landed by PRODUCT-RELAY-001 / PR #564. The FanRelay token is NOT in config/webflash-builds.json (no FanRelay build entry exists). The FanRelay config string is NOT in release_one_required_configs. No WebFlash wrapper exists under products/webflash/. The S360-KIT-BATH-RELAY kit stays future-expansion / hardware-pending / webflash_exposure_allowed_now: false / stable_ready_now: false. WEBFLASH-RELAY-001, RELEASE-RELAY-001, and WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001 stay blocked behind their own gates. MANUAL-FIRMWARE-CANDIDATE-001 (2026-05-27) records compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile for this top-level FanRelay product compile-only target from the already-committed FW-FULL-COMPILE-TOPLEVEL-FANS-001 / PR #616 full-lane run (this product YAML was target #8, rc=0, Flash 53.8% / 987219 bytes); it is recorded from that committed evidence, not a new compile run, and none is fabricated. This makes the FanRelay top-level product YAML a manual / no-WebFlash firmware candidate alongside FanPWM / FanDAC. It stays compile-only: NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT hardware-stable, NOT compliance / competent-person approval, NOT kit-default / recommended. HOSTED-CI CORROBORATION (RELEASE-PREVIEW-COMPILE-RESULTS-001): the hosted Preview Compile Dry-Run (run 26821900127; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; 2026-06-02; ESPHome 2026.4.5; job 'Compile Dry-Run: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanRelay-RoomIQ') re-compiled this product YAML on a hosted GitHub Actions runner and it passed (conclusion success), corroborating the existing validated-full-compile status on hosted CI. The compile_validation_status value is unchanged; this is firmware-build proof only and adds no hardware / bench / compliance / stable claim and no WebFlash exposure / release artifact.

targets.notes: Bathroom DAC room-bundle (S360-KIT-BATH-P-DAC). REQUIRED FanDAC I2C address override: the bundle relocates the second GP8403 (IC2) from its 0x59 package default to 0x5A (fan_dac_2_i2c_address) so it does not collide with the air-quality SGP41 at 0x59 on the shared core_i2c bus; the installer must set the IC2 DIP switch (SW2) to 0x5A to match. DIP-position -> address bench verification is owed by FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001. ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 registered this full-composition room-bundle fan-control product YAML into the compile-only validation lane. compile_validation_status is validated-full-compile: the hosted Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (.github/workflows/compile-only.yml; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; run 26913592989; 2026-06-04; ref main; ESPHome 2026.4.5) ran a real esphome compile against this product YAML and it passed (Metadata Validation success; Full ESPHome Compile success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; no compile is fabricated. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT hardware / bench / compliance proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion. No config/webflash-builds.json row, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name. webflash_exposure_allowed_now stays false; shipment_status stays compile-only.

targets.webflash_grammar_excluded_reason: AirIQ + FanDAC is intentionally absent from the WebFlash one-click firmware-combination grammar: the fandac_conflicts_with_airiq token mutex (config/webflash-compatibility.json) keeps AirIQ + FanDAC out of the matrix because the GP8403 second DAC defaults to 0x59 (the AirIQ SGP41 address) and the one-click WebFlash surface cannot set the required IC2 DIP switch to 0x5A. This room-bundle preview composes the 0x5A address override (fan_dac_2_i2c_address) and documents the required manual DIP-switch setting, so it is the documented address-overridden exception, compile-validated outside the one-click grammar. Bench verification of the DIP-position -> address mapping is owed by FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001.
targets.notes: Kitchen DAC room-bundle (S360-KIT-KITCHEN-P-DAC). REQUIRED FanDAC I2C address override: the bundle relocates the second GP8403 (IC2) from its 0x59 package default to 0x5A (fan_dac_2_i2c_address) so it does not collide with the air-quality SGP41 at 0x59 on the shared core_i2c bus; the installer must set the IC2 DIP switch (SW2) to 0x5A to match. DIP-position -> address bench verification is owed by FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001. This AirIQ + FanDAC config stays OUT of the one-click WebFlash firmware-combination matrix (the fandac_conflicts_with_airiq token mutex excludes AirIQ + FanDAC from the WebFlash easy-mode grammar, which cannot encode the DIP-switch override); this advanced / manual room-bundle preview is the documented address-overridden exception. ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 registered this full-composition room-bundle fan-control product YAML into the compile-only validation lane. compile_validation_status is validated-full-compile: the hosted Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (.github/workflows/compile-only.yml; workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full; run 26913592989; 2026-06-04; ref main; ESPHome 2026.4.5) ran a real esphome compile against this product YAML and it passed (Metadata Validation success; Full ESPHome Compile success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; no compile is fabricated. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT hardware / bench / compliance proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion. No config/webflash-builds.json row, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name. webflash_exposure_allowed_now stays false; shipment_status stays compile-only.

targets.reason: FanDAC (S360-312) compile-only validation target (FW-COMPILE-DAC-001). Exercises the FanDAC package composition (two GP8403 dual-channel I2C DACs, four neutral outputs; canonical alias packages/expansions/fan_dac.yaml -> fan_gp8403.yaml) on the proven Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health base after PACKAGE-DAC-001 / PR #573 implemented the package and CORE-ABSTRACT-BUS-001B landed the core_i2c bus the GP8403 peripheral binds. This target resolves the gp8403 voltage enum concern raised by PR #574: the package fan_dac_1_output_range / fan_dac_2_output_range substitutions are fixed from the invalid ESPHome enum string '0-10V' to the valid '10V' (customer-facing 0-10V) per https://esphome.io/components/output/gp8403, matching the default '10V' the output-range policy already implied (docs/hardware/s360-312-r4-fandac.md row 6). The full ESPHome compile is validated by run 26364679370 (workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full, 9 compile-only targets, conclusion success): scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile ran against this YAML in CI and passed. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a PRODUCT-DAC-001 / WEBFLASH-DAC-001 / RELEASE-DAC-001 unblock, NOT S360-312 schematic / BOM verification, NOT hardware proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: FanDAC compile-only skeleton lives at products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fandac.yaml (NOT under products/webflash/, NOT a top-level products/ product YAML). No config/product-catalog.json entry is added (the products/compile-only/ subdirectory is excluded from the top-level catalog enumeration gate), so NO normal DAC product YAML is added; PRODUCT-DAC-001 stays gated on this validation. The FanDAC token is NOT in config/webflash-builds.json (no FanDAC build entry exists). The Ceiling-POE-FanDAC config string is NOT in release_one_required_configs. No WebFlash wrapper exists under products/webflash/. No webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records that the full ESPHome compile passed in run 26364679370 (workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full, 9 targets, conclusion success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; voltage_enum_fixed: true records that the gp8403 voltage enum concern is resolved at the package layer (10V, customer-facing 0-10V), not merely tracked. A single GP8403 cannot drive one output at 0-5V and the other at 0-10V (one V5V reference / one range register per chip); this target does not claim that.

targets.reason: FanPWM (S360-311) compile-only validation target (FW-COMPILE-PWM-001). Exercises the PWM-drive-only FanPWM package composition (four independent SX1509 PWM-drive fan-speed controllers; canonical packages/expansions/fan_pwm.yaml composing the neutral binding packages/expansions/fan_pwm_sx1509.yaml) on the proven Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health base after PACKAGE-PWM-001-IMPLEMENT-001 / PR #590 reconciled the package to the PWM-drive-only scope and CORE-ABSTRACT-BUS-001B landed the core_i2c bus the SX1509 expander binds. Validates that the package composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version: the SX1509 hub on core_i2c, the four output: platform: sx1509 PWM-drive outputs (fan_pwm_drive_1..4 on channels 0..3), and the four fan: platform: speed controllers (fan_pwm_1..4). The package wires NO pulse_counter and NO RPM sensor (an SX1509 expander pin is compile-proven unable to back an ESPHome pulse_counter per PWM-SX1509-TACH-PROOF-001 / PR #589; esphome config rejects it with '[sx1509] is an invalid option for [pin]'); the four Pul_Cou1..4 lines stay as the binding's INTERNAL diagnostic binary GPIO states (fan_pwm_tach_1..4), never surfaced as RPM. TachIO / GPIO16 stays reserved/pending. The full ESPHome compile is validated by run 26414398902 (Compile-only Firmware Validation; compile_mode=full, 10 compile-only targets, conclusion success): the 'Run compile-only validator (full compile)' step ran scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile against this YAML in CI and passed. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a PRODUCT-PWM-001 / WEBFLASH-PWM-001 / RELEASE-PWM-001 unblock, NOT S360-311 schematic / BOM / bench (PWM polarity, current/thermal envelope) verification, NOT RPM support, NOT hardware proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion.

targets.reason: FanPWM (S360-311) NATIVE ESP32-S3 GPIO compile-only validation target (S360-311-NATIVE-FANPWM-YAML-001). Exercises the NATIVE FanPWM package composition (packages/expansions/fan_pwm_native.yaml) on the proven Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health base: four native output: platform: ledc PWM-drive outputs (TachPMW1..4 -> native ESP32-S3 IO10 / IO11 / IO12 / IO39) feeding four fan: platform: speed controllers, plus three native sensor: platform: pulse_counter tach inputs (Pul_Cou1 / Pul_Cou2 / Pul_Cou4 -> native ESP32-S3 IO17 / IO18 / IO9) as INTERNAL diagnostic pulse-rate inputs only. This is the native-GPIO successor to the legacy / superseded SX1509 composition: it does NOT use the SX1509 for PWM output and does NOT use the SX1509 for any tach / pulse_counter input, per the canonical map in docs/hardware/s360-100-native-fan-gpio-map.md (S360-100-NATIVE-FAN-GPIO-MAP-001) established by PR #634 / #637 / #638 / #639. Pul_Cou3 (native IO46) is DISABLED / TBD because IO46 collides with the Core fan_status_led_pin (GPIO46); TachIO (native IO16) is RESERVED / PENDING (ambiguous shared-passthrough role). No GPIO numbers are invented; every pin is the schematic-printed value recorded by S360-100-NATIVE-FAN-GPIO-MAP-001 (none bench-verified). Compile coverage is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a WEBFLASH-PWM-001 / RELEASE-PWM-001 / WF-IMPORT-PWM-001 unblock, NOT S360-311 schematic / BOM / bench (PWM polarity, current/thermal envelope) verification, NOT RPM / tach support, NOT hardware proof, NOT compliance approval, and NOT stable / preview promotion.

The legacy SX1509 full-compile run 26414398902 does NOT transfer to this native composition; this row is the native composition's own compile proof. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile is compile coverage ONLY: NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT WebFlash import, NOT a release artifact, NOT compliance / competent-person approval, and NOT hardware-stable readiness — webflash_exposure_allowed_now stays false and shipment_status stays compile-only. rpm_supported: false records that the native pulse_counter inputs are internal diagnostic pulse-rate inputs only — no per-fan or aggregate RPM is surfaced and no RPM claim is made; RPM / tach support stays UNVALIDATED until measured bench evidence exists (a green compile is NOT RPM / tach bench validation). targets.reason: FanTRIAC (S360-320) compile-only validation target (TRIAC-UNBLOCK-BUILD-001). The S360-100-R4 Core respin removed the SX1509 expander and routes the J15 TRIAC connector nets TRI_GPIO1/TRI_GPIO2 DIRECT to interrupt-capable ESP32-S3 GPIOs (gate IO13, zero-cross IO14), resolving the HW-005 BUILDABILITY blocker (the old GPIO5/GPIO6 placeholders collided with RoomIQ SEN0609 on J10, and the connector previously routed via the SX1509, which ESPHome ac_dimmer cannot use). This target validates that the FanTRIAC product YAML composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly with the real ac_dimmer pin mapping. Compile success is firmware-build proof of BUILDABILITY ONLY: NOT bench verification, NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT a default kit / recommended, and NOT COMPLIANCE-001 mains-voltage sign-off. Stable stays gated by COMPLIANCE-001 (+ PACKAGE-TRIAC-001).

compile_evidence.ci_dispatch_note: Hosted compile-only.yml (compile_mode=full) is the canonical corroboration lane; workflow_dispatch was not available to the authoring integration, so no hosted CI run id is claimed (mirrors the FW-FULL-COMPILE-TOPLEVEL-FANS-001 local-run precedent). A maintainer dispatch of compile-only.yml corroborates on hosted CI. No compile is fabricated.

targets.notes: FanTRIAC product YAML lives at products/sense360-ceiling-poe-ventiq-fantriac-roomiq.yaml (top-level; NOT under products/webflash/ or products/compile-only/). blocked: true marks that the FanTRIAC token stays OFF the WebFlash build matrix and out of one-click exposure (the FanTRIAC guardrail in scripts/validate_compile_targets.py + tests/test_compile_targets.py); it does NOT mean the YAML is unbuildable. TRIAC-UNBLOCK-BUILD-001 proves it compiles after the SX1509-free Core respin routes TRI_GPIO1/TRI_GPIO2 direct to IO13/IO14 (gate IO13, zero-cross IO14). The matching catalog row in config/product-catalog.json (config_string: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanTRIAC-RoomIQ) moves from status: blocked / HW-005 to status: compile-only with the build_blocker cleared and stable_blocker = COMPLIANCE-001 + PACKAGE-TRIAC-001. No config/webflash-builds.json row, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no webflash_wrapper exposure. FanTRIAC stays not stable, not recommended, not default, not buyable, not auto-imported; WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001 (downstream WebFlash import behind the advanced acknowledgement) stays a separate follow-up. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records the local full esphome compile (ESPHome 2026.4.5; success); no hosted CI run id is claimed.
